chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: Events are actions that happen in the browser, like clicking a button or typing in a field.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: JavaScript lets you respond to these events and run code when they occur.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The addEventListener method attaches an event handler to an element.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The first parameter is the event type like click, mouseover, keydown.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The second parameter is the function that will be executed when the event occurs.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The click event fires when an element is clicked.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The mouseover event fires when the mouse pointer moves over an element.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The mouseout event fires when the mouse pointer moves out of an element.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The keydown event fires when a key is pressed on the keyboard.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The keyup event fires when a key is released on the keyboard.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The submit event fires when a form is submitted.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The change event fires when the value of an input element changes.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The input event fires every time the value of an input element changes.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The event object is automatically passed to the event handler function.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The event object contains useful information like the target element and event type.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: event.preventDefault prevents the default behavior of an event, like form submission.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: event.stopPropagation stops the event from bubbling up to parent elements.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: Event bubbling means events propagate from the target element up to the root.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: Event delegation is a technique where you add one event listener to a parent element.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: Event delegation is more efficient than adding listeners to every child element.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: The DOMContentLoaded event fires when the HTML document has been completely loaded.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: Always wait for DOMContentLoaded before manipulating the DOM in your scripts.

chunks.title: JavaScript Events & Event Listeners
chunks.text: Events are the heart of interactive web applications and you will use them constantly.

text: Events are actions that happen in the browser, like clicking a button or typing in a field. JavaScript lets you respond to these events and run code when they occur. The addEventListener method attaches an event handler to an element. The first parameter is the event type like click, mouseover, keydown. The second parameter is the function that will be executed when the event occurs. The click event fires when an element is clicked. The mouseover event fires when the mouse pointer moves over an element. The mouseout event fires when the mouse pointer moves out of an element. The keydown event fires when a key is pressed on the keyboard. The keyup event fires when a key is released on the keyboard. The submit event fires when a form is submitted. The change event fires when the value of an input element changes. The input event fires every time the value of an input element changes. The event object is automatically passed to the event handler function. The event object contains useful information like the target element and event type. event.preventDefault prevents the default behavior of an event, like form submission. event.stopPropagation stops the event from bubbling up to parent elements. Event bubbling means events propagate from the target element up to the root. Event delegation is a technique where you add one event listener to a parent element. Event delegation is more efficient than adding listeners to every child element. The DOMContentLoaded event fires when the HTML document has been completely loaded. Always wait for DOMContentLoaded before manipulating the DOM in your scripts. Events are the heart of interactive web applications and you will use them constantly.
